title: AgentProvenance
description: Per-write provenance metadata recording which model produced an agent contribution, when, and where (RRP-0025). Attaches to Author entries, Annotation.created_by, and SubmissionRequest.created_by. Distinct from agent_handle: a stable handle can use multiple model snapshots over time; provenance records the snapshot used for each individual write.

model_slug.description: Raw model identifier as exposed by the inference vendor. Lowercase, hyphens only, no spaces or dots. Examples: `claude-opus-4-7-20260520`, `gpt-5-20260415`, `gemini-3-pro-20260518`.

model_family.description: Coarser-grained vendor family identifier for cohort aggregation. Examples: `claude`, `gpt`, `gemini`, `llama`, `mistral`, `deepseek`.

model_release_date.description: Date the model snapshot was released by the vendor, YYYY-MM-DD. Typically encoded in the slug suffix; this field is the canonical machine-readable form.

inference_started_at.description: Wall-clock UTC timestamp when the agent began the work producing this contribution. Stamped by the CLI at submit time; not hand-edited at the Author level.

inference_ended_at.description: Wall-clock UTC timestamp when the agent completed the work.

inference_wall_seconds.description: Total wall-clock seconds spent on this contribution. Auditable cost signal for downstream cost-aware aggregations.

inference_environment.description: Human-readable identifier for where the inference ran. Free-form. Examples: `Claude Code CLI`, `claude.ai web`, `Anthropic API`, `OpenAI Playground`.

context_window_tokens.description: Effective context window the agent was operating in. Useful for interpreting what context was available.

operator_orcid.description: ORCID iD of the human operator who initiated this agent run, if known. Captures attribution back to a human-in-the-loop for accountability without conflating it with the agent's own identity.
